alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our modeling approach can be applied to other species that produce medicinally or industrially useful compounds. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This forward-looking assertion about cross-species application does not appear among the publication claims and thus appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Microbial community compositions were significantly different between asymptomatic and symptomatic bacteriuria, suggesting microbial community signatures have potential application as a diagnostic tool. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Microbial community compositions were significantly different between asymptomatic and symptomatic bacteriuria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core result but expresses it with higher stated certainty; the diagnostic potential is implied by the retained result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A significant decrease in local (alpha) diversity was noted in symptomatic bacteriuria compared to the asymptomatic bacteriuria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A significant decrease in local (alpha) diversity was noted in symptomatic bacteriuria compared to asymptomatic bacteriuria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific result with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining urine and catheter microbiome data improved the model performance during cross-validation, yielding a mean area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.91-0.98 for amplicon sequence variant (ASVs) counts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining urine and catheter microbiome data improved the model performance for classifying symptomatic and asymptomatic bacteriuria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion that combining data improves classification but omits the specific AUROC range and ASV detail, rephrasing the performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings demonstrate that signatures within catheter and urine microbiota could serve as tools to monitor the health status of SCI patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Signatures within catheter and urine microbiota could serve as tools to monitor the health status of SCI patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about monitoring health status using microbial signatures with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Establishing an early warning system based on these microbial signatures could equip physicians with alternative management strategies, potentially reducing UTI episodes and associated hospital costs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Establishing a classification system based on these microbial signatures could significantly improve patient quality of life while mitigating the impact of drug-resistant UTI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes an early-warning/management-focused claim into a broader classification-system claim emphasizing quality of life and drug-resistant UTI mitigation rather than explicit cost/episode reductions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The level of proactive control is associated with sustained frontal-midline theta activity preceding trial onset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The level of preparatory control is associated with sustained frontal-midline theta activity preceding trial onset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication uses 'preparatory' rather than 'proactive' but retains the same association and certainty, representing a terminological clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Discriminandum onset triggers a control prediction error signal reflected in a left frontal positivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Discriminandum onset triggers a control prediction error signal reflected in a left frontal positivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result is identically stated in the publication with the same certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reactive control scales with the prediction error and suppresses the neural representation of the discriminandum. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reflexive control that scales with the prediction error suppresses the neural representation of the discriminandum. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dynamic interplay of proactive and reactive control mitigates interference between perceptual and mnemonic representations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dynamic interplay of preparatory and reflexive control mitigates interference between perceptual and mnemonic representations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hercules is the first machine learning-based long read error correction algorithm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hercules is the first machine learning-based long read error correction algorithm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this novelty claim verbatim, leaving it unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hercules models every long read as a profile Hidden Markov Model with respect to the underlying platform’s error profile. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific technical phrasing about modeling each read as a profile HMM is not present among the listed publication claims and thus appears omitted from the claim list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hercules-corrected reads have the highest mapping rate among all competing algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hercules-corrected reads have the highest mapping rate among all competing algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical performance claim directly, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hercules achieves the highest accuracy when most of the basepairs of a long read are covered with short reads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hercules achieves the highest accuracy when the breadth of coverage is high. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same dependence on coverage but uses broader phrasing ('breadth of coverage'), clarifying the condition for high accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine-learning methods can effectively identify reproductive phasiRNAs in rice and maize from other small RNAs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine-learning methods can effectively identify reproductive phasiRNAs in rice and maize from other small RNAs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this main result directly, with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification performance of the Random Forest classifiers achieved over 80% in accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and positive predicted value. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification performance of the developed Random Forest classifiers is greater than 80% in accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and positive predicted value. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same quantitative classifier-performance claim with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PhasiRNAs exhibit strand specificity and position-specific nucleotide biases that may influence AGO sorting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PhasiRNAs exhibit strand specificity and position-specific nucleotide biases that may influence AGO sorting. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified 5’-end features provide insights into AGO-phasiRNA interactions and suggest a model of competition for AGO loading. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified 5'-end features provide insights into AGO-phasiRNA interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly retains the 'insights' portion of the preprint claim; the model suggestion is separated out into an additional, more tentative claim (see next alignment).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified 5’-end features provide insights into AGO-phasiRNA interactions and suggest a model of competition for AGO loading. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hypothetical model of competition for AGO loading between phasiRNAs of different nucleotide compositions is proposed. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly frames the model as a hypothetical proposal with lower certainty; this preserves the preprint's suggested model but with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GenEpi is a computational package developed to uncover epistasis associated with phenotypes using a machine learning approach. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GenEpi is a computational package designed to uncover epistasis associated with phenotypes using a machine learning approach. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GenEpi identifies both within-gene and cross-gene epistasis through a two-stage modeling workflow. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GenEpi effectively and efficiently detects epistasis associated with phenotypes, as demonstrated by results on simulation data and Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulated data showed that GenEpi outperforms other widely-used methods in detecting ground-truth epistasis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GenEpi outperforms other widely-used methods in detecting ground-truth epistasis based on simulated data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GenEpi reveals the capability to find disease-related variants and variant interactions that have biological meanings and predictive power. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GenEpi effectively and efficiently detects epistasis associated with phenotypes, as demonstrated by results on simulation data and Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides disease-specific demonstration (Alzheimer's) that strengthens the preprint's claim about biologically meaningful and predictive interactions, increasing certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine-learning based approach utilizing mid-infrared spectra can accurately characterize both age and species identity of female Anopheles mosquitoes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate a machine-learning based approach that uses mid-infrared spectra of mosquitoes to characterise simultaneously both age and species identity of females of the African malaria vector species Anopheles gambiae and An. arabiensis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction of age structures using this method is statistically indistinguishable from true modelled distributions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mid-infrared spectroscopy-based prediction of mosquito age structures was statistically indistinguishable from true modelled distributions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This technique is substantially faster and has a negligible cost per mosquito compared to current methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method does not require highly trained personnel and can be easily applied in both laboratory and field settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method is a promising alternative to current mosquito species and age-grading approaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With larger mid-infrared spectroscopy data sets, the technique can be further improved and expanded to other disease vectors. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further improvements to accuracy and expansion for use with other mosquito vectors are possible through collection of larger mid-infrared spectroscopy data sets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach for predicting MICs is stable over time, despite annual fluctuations in antimicrobial resistance gene content in the sampled genomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate a repeatable, semi-automated method to rapidly clean SFCM data from a phytoplankton community by removing signals that do not belong to live phytoplankton cells. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An automated high-content image analysis pipeline was developed for image-based cell profiling in the organoid system. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurate identification of transcription units (TUs) is crucial for delineating transcriptional regulatory networks in prokaryotic genomes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing tools for predicting TUs based on bacterial RNA-seq data and genome sequences are insufficient, indicating a need for improved methods. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The R package rSeqTU provides a comprehensive pipeline for TU identification, including functionalities for quality control, mapping, feature selection, prediction, and visualization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: rSeqTU employs a random forest algorithm for feature selection and support vector machine (SVM) for building TU prediction models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: rSeqTU employs a random forest algorithm for feature selection and support vector machine (SVM) for building TU prediction models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The genomic distance to the Xist locus is the prime determinant of the speed of gene silencing. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pre-marking of gene promoters with polycomb complexes is associated with fast silencing. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Features associated with active transcription and the O-GlcNAc transferase Ogt are enriched at rapidly silenced genes. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies important features and feature blocks for predicting bitter-sweet taste using different sets of molecular descriptors. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All datasets and BitterSweet models are made publicly available to facilitate future research in bitter-sweet taste prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All datasets and BitterSweet models are made publicly available to facilitate future research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep learning based method has been developed to measure the similarity between colorectal cancer tumors and other tumors or disease models such as cancer cell lines. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method efficiently leverages multi-omics data sets to learn latent factors that describe the data in lower dimension. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Nanopore sequencing has significant promise in fields like transcriptomics and other omics studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement about nanopore promise is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The limited accuracy in base identification of nanopore sequencing has restricted its widespread application. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The limited accuracy in base identification of nanopore sequencing has restricted its widespread application. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is carried over verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Advancements in RNA sequencing remain limited despite many algorithms developed to improve DNA decoding. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Advancements in RNA sequencing remain limited despite many algorithms developed to improve DNA decoding. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The observation about limited RNA sequencing advances relative to DNA decoding algorithms is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The general neural-network contraction model characterizes the impacts of nonsynonymous single nucleotide variants (SNVs) on human health. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The general neural-network contraction model characterizes single residue variants (SRVs) impacts on human health. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication uses the more specific term SRVs in place of SNVs, clarifying the variant granularity while preserving the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The nascent digital twin interprets SNV implications to discover disease mechanisms and evaluate potential remedies for efficacy without using animal models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nascent digital twin interprets SRV implications for disease mechanism discovery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the digital twin's role in mechanism discovery but omits claims about evaluating remedies and explicit replacement of animal models, narrowing and toning down the original capability statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology called 2-dimensional correlation genetics (2D-CG) maps co-domains that couple human genetic diversity to the βmys/MYBPC3 complex. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model rationalizes phenotype and pathogenicity assignment based on SRV characteristics, decoding βmys/MYBPC3 complex genetics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the 2D-CG mapping into a model that decodes βmys/MYBPC3 genetics and rationalizes phenotype assignment, making the implication more explicit and stated with higher confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural-symbolic hybrid model integrates genetic and protein interaction data into a digital twin that evolves by processing new information content. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model rationalizes phenotype and pathogenicity assignment based on SRV characteristics, decoding βmys/MYBPC3 complex genetics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the model's interpretive and decoding functions for SRVs and βmys/MYBPC3 rather than the broader claims about integration of protein interaction data and an evolving digital twin, reducing scope and toning the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a method to compress genotyping data involving single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) while preserving non-linear relations among variants. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a method to compress single nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) data while leveraging the linkage disequilibrium (LD) structure and preserving potential epistasis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the method description more specific (mentioning LD and epistasis) while preserving the original contribution to compress SNP data and retain nonlinear relations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our autoencoder-based approach leads to more than 99% average test reconstruction accuracy while compressing the input to nearly 10% of the original size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieved 99% average test reconstruction accuracy while compressing the input to nearly 10% of the original size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance claim is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Haplotype-block based autoencoders outperform their linear alternative Principal Component Analysis (PCA) by approximately 3% chromosome-wide accuracy of reconstructed variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Haplotype-block based autoencoders outperform linear Principal Component Analysis (PCA) by approximately 3% chromosome-wide accuracy of reconstructed variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance against PCA is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach is the first to simultaneously leverage genome haplotype structure and deep neural networks for dimensionality reduction of genetic data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach is the first to simultaneously leverage haplotype structure and DNNs for dimensionality reduction of genetic data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is preserved in the published paper with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DNNs explain a significant portion of variance in neural data but do not fully capture dynamic representations in higher-level human visual cortex. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DNNs explain a significant portion of variance in neural data but do not fully capture dynamic representations in higher-level human visual cortex. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion about DNN explanatory limits is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Visuo-semantic models can explain variance in neural timeseries data beyond the explanatory power of DNNs alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Visuo-semantic models can explain variance in neural dynamics beyond the explanatory power of DNNs alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result that visuo-semantic features add explanatory power beyond DNNs is restated with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lower-level visual areas are better explained by DNN features during the early phase of the response, while higher-level cortical dynamics are best accounted for by visuo-semantic features during a later time window. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lower-level visual areas are better explained by DNN features starting early in time, while higher-level cortical dynamics are best accounted for by visuo-semantic features starting later in time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The temporal dissociation claim is preserved but reported with higher certainty in the publication than in the preprint, increasing stated confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Object parts and basic categories among visuo-semantic features drive the advantage over DNNs in explaining higher-level cortical dynamics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Object parts and basic categories among visuo-semantic features drive the advantage over DNNs in explaining higher-level cortical dynamics. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The explanatory drivers are the same, but the publication reports this result with lower certainty than the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DNNs and humans may rely on different object features for visual recognition, suggesting a path toward more accurate models of ventral stream computations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DNNs and humans may rely on different object features for visual recognition, suggesting a path toward more accurate models of ventral-stream computations. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conceptual suggestion is unchanged but published with reduced certainty (low vs medium), so the confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: State-of-the-art deep, feed-forward convolutional ANNs accurately predicted primate patterns of object-level confusion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: State-of-the-art deep, feedforward convolutional ANNs accurately predicted primate patterns of object-level confusion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result with effectively identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All tested DCNN IC models were significantly non-predictive of primate performance for individual images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All tested DCNN_IC models were significantly nonpredictive of primate performance for individual images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same image-level non-predictivity result with equivalent strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction failure of DCNN IC models was not accounted for by simple image attributes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction failure of DCNN_IC models was not accounted for by simple image attributes nor rescued by simple model modifications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the original point but explicitly extends it to note that simple model modifications also do not rescue the failure, broadening the conditions covered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New ANN models are needed to more precisely capture the neural mechanisms underlying primate object vision. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current DCNN_IC models cannot account for the image-level behavioral patterns of primates. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the implication slightly differently—stating current models cannot account for image-level behavior—supporting the preprint's call for new models but with a different emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High-resolution, large-scale primate behavioral benchmarks could serve as direct guides for discovering better ANN models of the primate visual system. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large-scale, high-resolution primate behavioral benchmarks could serve as direct guides for discovering better ANN models of the primate visual system. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution/suggestion with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A high-throughput computer-vision method was developed to measure the internal structure of cranberry fruit. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A high-throughput computer-vision method was developed to measure the internal structure of cranberry fruit. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The internal structure of cranberry fruit may influence fruit firmness. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the potential use of internal structure and firmness data as a decision-making tool for cranberry processing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from a cautious biological influence claim to an applied recommendation for decision-making, shifting the emphasis and broadening the implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Correlations between firmness and internal morphological characteristics were explored using data from 16 cranberry cultivars over a 40-day period. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The internal structure data paired with fruit firmness values allowed exploration of correlations between firmness and internal morphological characteristics. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the same experimental activity as an exploration enabled by paired data but presents it with somewhat reduced asserted certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This study introduces novel methods to define key parameters of cranberry fruit that have not been characterized before. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit claim about novel parameter definition that is not separately asserted in the preprint text.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SiGra outperformed state-of-the-art methods on both single-cell spatial profiles and spot-level spatial transcriptomics data from complex tissues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SiGra outperforms state-of-the-art methods on both single-cell and spot-level spatial transcriptomics data from complex tissues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the primary performance result with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of immunohistochemistry images improved the model performance by 37% (95%CI: 27% – 50%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inclusion of immunohistochemistry images improves the model performance by 37% (95% CI: 27–50%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the quantified improvement claim identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SiGra improves the characterization of intratumor heterogeneity and intercellular communications in human lung cancer samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SiGra improves the characterization of intratumor heterogeneity and intercellular communication. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the tissue-specific preprint claim to intratumor heterogeneity broadly while lowering the stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SiGra recovers the known microscopic anatomy in both human brain and mouse liver tissues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SiGra effectively integrates different spatial modality data to gain deep insights into spatial cellular ecosystems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes general integration and insight into spatial ecosystems rather than the specific recovery of known anatomy in particular tissues, shifting and softening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepUrfold leverages its deep generative model's learned embeddings to provide a nuanced exploration of fold space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepUrfold learns representations that define superfamilies of proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the learned representations' role in defining superfamilies, a more specific framing of the preprint's description of embeddings enabling exploration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deploying DeepUrfold with CATH suggests a new, mostly-continuous view of fold space that integrates sequence, structure, and function properties. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepUrfold suggests a mostly-continuous view of fold space that extends beyond simple geometric similarity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core claim about a mostly-continuous view but softens certainty and shifts emphasis away from explicitly listing all integrated property types.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepUrfold can quantitatively represent and detect evolutionarily-remote relationships that evade existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deploying DeepUrfold with CATH reveals evolutionarily-remote relationships that evade existing methodologies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this capability essentially unchanged, including the emphasis on detecting remote relationships missed by other methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The discrete views of fold space may inherently miss many remote relationships due to their binning of similar folds into distinct groups. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The discrete/continuous dichotomy of fold space remains unresolved. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's limitation as a broader unresolved dichotomy, softening the specific mechanistic claim but keeping the same cautionary point.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The conductance-based neural mass mean-field models can investigate laminar-specific and GABAergic mechanisms of the auditory response. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The conductance-based neural mass mean-field models can investigate laminar-specific and GABAergic mechanisms of the auditory response. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the modeling capability claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neuronal model accurately recapitulated the observed magnetoencephalographic data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neuronal model accurately recapitulated the observed magnetoencephalographic data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the model's fit to MEG data with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tiagabine affects GABAergic modulation of deep pyramidal and interneuronal cell populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tiagabine affects GABAergic modulation of deep pyramidal and interneuronal cell populations, with effects transitioning from auditory cortex in standard trials to prefrontal cortex in deviant trials. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core effect but adds trial-dependent spatial detail and reduces stated certainty, clarifying and extending the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of pharmaco-magnetoencephalography with dynamic causal models provides a platform to evaluate the effects of disease and pharmacological interventions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of pharmaco-magnetoencephalography with dynamic causal models of frontotemporal networks provides a platform to evaluate the effects of disease and pharmacological interventions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the scope by specifying frontotemporal networks while keeping the same overall conclusion and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SPP amplitudes did not increase as cloze probability increased, suggesting that the component may not reflect prediction during natural language processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Semantic prediction potential amplitudes did not increase as cloze probability increased, suggesting that the component may not reflect prediction during natural language processing.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical finding with slightly different terminology and a higher stated certainty, so the claim content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Initial N400 results at the article provided evidence against phonological prediction in language.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Initial N400 results at the article provided evidence against phonological prediction in language, in line with Nieuwland and colleagues' findings.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the original result but adds contextualization (cite/consistency with prior work), which clarifies rather than changes the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increases in article surprisal were associated with increased N400 amplitudes, consistent with prediction accounts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increases in article surprisal were associated with increased N400 amplitudes, consistent with prediction accounts.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim, so the semantic content, scope, and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The relationship between surprisal and N400 amplitude was not observed when the surprisal of the two prior words was low.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The relationship between surprisal and N400 amplitude was not observed when the surprisal of the two prior words was low, suggesting that expectation violations at the article may be overlooked under highly predictable conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the empirical pattern and adds an interpretive sentence; this is a clarification rather than a substantive revision of scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The present study extends upon existing neurocognitive models of language and prediction by illuminating the flexible and subject-specific nature of predictive processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The present study extends upon existing neurocognitive models of language and prediction by illuminating the flexible and subject-specific nature of predictive processing.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is preserved in content but the publication expresses it with lower certainty, indicating a tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multidimensional semantic information was successfully reconstructed from activity patterns across visual and lateral parietal cortices during both viewing and recalling scenes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multidimensional semantic information was successfully reconstructed from activity patterns across visual and lateral parietal cortices during both viewing and recalling scenes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this primary result with no meaningful change in content, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Visual cortical reconstructions were much more accurate when images were viewed compared to when they were recalled from memory, while lateral parietal reconstructions were comparably accurate across both conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Visual cortical reconstructions were much more accurate when images were viewed compared to when they were recalled from memory, while lateral parietal reconstructions were comparably accurate across both conditions.
alignments.rationale: The detailed comparison across regions and conditions is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: fMRI-based reconstructions reliably matched subjects’ verbal descriptions of their memories, with reconstructions from ventral temporal cortex more closely matching subjects’ own verbal recall than that of other subjects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: fMRI-based reconstructions reliably matched subjects' verbal descriptions of their memories, with reconstructions from ventral temporal cortex more closely matching subjects' own verbal recall than that of other subjects.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this finding without meaningful change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Encoding models reliably transferred across subjects, allowing for successful reconstruction of memories using models trained on data from entirely independent subjects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Encoding models reliably transferred across subjects, allowing successful reconstruction of memories using models trained on data from entirely independent subjects.
alignments.rationale: The cross-subject transfer result is retained as-is in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LIMPACAT achieved approximately 80% accuracy in classifying immune cell levels relevant to patient prognosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LIMPACAT exhibited ~80% accuracy in classifying immune cell levels from HCC WSIs.
alignments.rationale: The reported performance (≈80% accuracy) is the same; the publication clarifies the input data (HCC WSIs) but does not alter the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study confirmed alignment in immune cell composition estimates between bulk RNA-seq and scRNA-seq.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is strong concordance between model prediction and deconvolution-derived estimates of immune cell compositions.
alignments.rationale: The preprint claimed alignment specifically between bulk RNA-seq and scRNA-seq estimates, whereas the publication emphasizes concordance between model predictions and deconvolution estimates, a different (narrower/shifted) comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LIMPACAT leverages whole-slide images to predict immune cell levels associated with prognosis in liver cancer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study introduces a deep learning framework that leverages whole-slide images to predict immune cell levels relevant to hepatocellular carcinoma prognosis.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution with consistent meaning but presents it with higher stated certainty (and specific naming), so the claim content is unchanged with amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of WSIs and multi-omics data enhances immune profiling and prognostic predictions in liver cancer research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: WSIs can serve as a proxy for immune profiling, facilitating pathology-based tumor microenvironment assessment.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized integration of WSIs with multi-omics to enhance profiling; the publication instead emphasizes WSIs alone as a proxy, which narrows the original integration claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Latent Cellular Analysis (LCA) is a machine learning-based analytical pipeline for characterizing cell-to-cell variation and dynamics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Latent Cellular Analysis (LCA) is a machine learning-based analytical pipeline developed to analyze single-cell RNA sequencing data.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the data domain (scRNA-seq) for the pipeline, which clarifies the original, more general contribution statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LCA features a dual-space model search with inference of latent cellular states and control of technical variations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LCA provides heuristic solutions for population number inference, dimension reduction, feature selection, and control of technical variations without explicit gene filtering.
alignments.rationale: The publication elaborates on the specific heuristics and procedures underlying the pipeline's features, clarifying the initially higher-level description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LCA has proved to be robust, accurate, scalable, and powerful in revealing subtle diversity in cell populations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LCA demonstrates robustness, accuracy, and power when compared with multiple state-of-the-art computational methods on large-scale real and simulated scRNA-seq data.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the performance claim but adds comparative evidence and dataset context, which clarifies and substantiates the original result.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The ability of LCA to learn from representative subsets of the data enhances its scalability for analyzing growing sample sizes in scRNA-seq data.
alignments.rationale: This publication-level statement about a specific mechanism for scalability (learning from representative subsets) is not explicitly present in the preprint and thus appears as an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Peptide-protein interactions can be modeled accurately using AlphaFold2.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2 enables quick and accurate modeling of peptide–protein interactions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the main result (accurate modeling of peptide–protein interactions) with comparable certainty and emphasis on speed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction of peptide-protein interactions is fast and does not require multiple sequence alignment information for the peptide partner.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our implementation of AlphaFold2 generates peptide–protein complex models without requiring multiple sequence alignment information for the peptide partner.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that peptide MSA is not required for modeling, restated as an implementation detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of AlphaFold2 for modeling peptide-protein interactions has the potential to significantly impact the study and manipulation of various peptide-protein complexes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2 holds great promise for providing structural insight into a wide range of peptide–protein complexes.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the forward-looking/conclusionary claim about broad potential impact with comparable certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of AlphaFold2 in modeling peptide-protein interactions was compared to the state-of-the-art peptide docking protocol PIPER-FlexPepDock.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly report the stated comparison to PIPER-FlexPepDock, so the specific comparative claim from the preprint appears to be omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2 can handle binding-induced conformational changes of the receptor.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts the ability to handle binding-induced conformational changes, a specific capability not explicitly stated in the preprint and therefore added in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CNNs trained on blurry to clear faces exhibit sustained robustness to blur. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNNs trained on blurry to clear faces observed sustained robustness to blur. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent scope and certainty; wording is effectively identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CNNs trained with blurry to clear objects fail to retain robustness to blur. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNNs trained with blurry to clear objects failed to retain robustness to blur. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim directly in content and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spatial frequency tuning of CNNs trained on faces is different from that of CNNs trained on objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spatial frequency tuning of the face-trained CNN was different from that of the object-trained CNN. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same specific finding about spatial frequency tuning with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Face recognition allows for more holistic processing compared to object recognition. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding claim about holistic processing appears in the published claims; the conclusion present in the preprint is omitted in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Blurry vision during infancy is insufficient to account for the robustness of adult vision to blurry objects. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Blurry vision during infancy is insufficient to account for the robustness of adult vision to blurry objects. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same conclusion verbatim, matching the preprint claim in scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WormNet, a convolutional neural network, can successfully classify C. elegans into long-lived and short-lived categories based on young adult images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: WormNet, a convolutional neural network, can successfully classify C. elegans into long-lived and short-lived categories based on young adult images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HydraNet CNNs allow for accurate segmentation of C. elegans into anterior, mid-body, and posterior parts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HydraNet CNNs allow for accurate segmentation of C. elegans into anterior, mid-body, and posterior parts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct correspondence between preprint and publication claim on segmentation accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The posterior part of the worm is identified as more important for classifying long-lived worms using a tandem segmentation-classification approach. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The posterior part of the worm is identified as more important for classifying long-lived worms using a tandem segmentation-classification approach. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the posterior part being more important is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed approach can accelerate anti-ageing drug discovery and aid in studying C. elegans pathologies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed approach can accelerate anti-aging drug discovery and enhance the study of C. elegans pathologies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's contribution claim matches the preprint's intent (minor wording differences only: 'aid' → 'enhance', spelling variant).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reaction times to touch are facilitated as a function of visual proximity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RTs to touch are facilitated as a function of visual proximity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical result with equivalent phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sigmoidal function describing the facilitation of reaction times shifts closer to the body if the immediately precedent trial had indexed a smaller visuo-tactile disparity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sigmoidal function describing the facilitation of RTs shifts closer to the body if the immediately precedent trial had indexed a smaller visuo-tactile disparity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific trial-history effect on the sigmoidal facilitation function is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Electroencephalographic correlates of peri-personal space are shaped by recent sensory history. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multisensory measure of PPS is shaped by recent sensory history. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's EEG-specific phrasing to a multisensory measure of PPS; this clarifies and broadens the claim beyond EEG alone.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that peri-personal space encoding remaps on a very rapid time-scale and is sensitive to recent sensory history. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PPS encoding remaps on a very rapid time-scale and is sensitive to sensory history. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about rapid remapping and sensitivity to recent sensory history.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A validated neural network model of PPS accounts for the present results via a simple Hebbian plasticity rule. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This modeling claim does not appear in the preprint claims list and is newly presented in the publication to account for empirical results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ISPS revealed two distinct networks synchronized during physical pain observation, one encompassing anterior insula and midcingulate regions and another encompassing parietal and inferior frontal regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ISPS revealed two distinct networks synchronized during physical pain observation, one encompassing anterior insula and midcingulate regions and another encompassing parietal and inferior frontal regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact correspondence between preprint and publication on the two synchronized networks during physical pain observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No robust network synchronization was observed while processing affective pain, possibly reflecting high inter-individual variation in response to socially transmitted pain experiences. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No robust network synchronization was observed for affective pain, possibly reflecting high inter-individual variation in response to socially transmitted pain experiences. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the null synchronization finding and the proposed interpretation unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ISPS approach permits segregation of networks engaged in different psychological processes, providing additional insight into shared neural mechanisms of empathy for physical pain, but not affective pain, across individuals. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ISPS approach permits segregation of networks engaged in different psychological processes, providing additional insight into shared neural mechanisms of empathy for physical pain, but not affective pain, across individuals. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The methodological and interpretive claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Over-parameterized models can provide a versatile, robust solution for learning a diverse set of functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Over-parameterized models can be simple and parsimonious, providing a versatile and robust solution for learning a diverse set of functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the characterization by adding 'simple and parsimonious' but preserves the core claim about versatility and robustness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These models do not learn an explicit, human-interpretable representation of the underlying structure of the data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The brain may use brute-force fitting processes similar to those observed in evolution to learn how to perceive and act upon the world. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the implication toward a hypothesis about brain learning processes rather than the stronger preprint assertion that models do not learn explicit interpretable representations, softening and broadening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Over-parameterized blind fitting presents a radical challenge to many of the underlying assumptions and practices in computational neuroscience and cognitive psychology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new family of direct-fit models challenges many theoretical assumptions in psychology and neuroscience. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the challenge-to-theory claim but frames it more strongly and broadly (higher certainty and as a contribution), effectively amplifying and clarifying the original preprint statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This shift in perspective informs longstanding debates and establishes unexpected links with evolution, ecological psychology, and artificial life. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This shift in perspective establishes unexpected links with developmental and ecological psychology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that the perspective establishes unexpected links but revises the specific linked fields (removing evolution and artificial life, adding developmental psychology), narrowing the scope of the originally listed domains.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advances in artificial neural networks demonstrate the power of optimizing millions of synaptic weights over millions of observations for robust real-world operation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific empirical statement about recent ANN advances is expressed in the publication but not listed among the preprint claims, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UNI-EM is a software program that enables 2D- and 3D-DNN-based segmentation for non-computer experts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: UNI-EM is a new software program developed for 2D and 3D CNN-based segmentation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent terminology (DNN vs CNN) and no substantive change in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 2D- and 3D-DNNs included in UNI-EM demonstrate state-of-the-art level segmentation performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 2D- and 3D-CNNs incorporated in UNI-EM demonstrate state-of-the-art level segmentation performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over verbatim aside from DNN/CNN wording, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Users can benefit from DNN-based segmentation without any knowledge of Python programming or DNN frameworks by following the provided example workflows. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Users can benefit from CNN-based segmentation using UNI-EM without needing knowledge of Python programming or CNN frameworks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same user-facing conclusion (with CNN terminology) and retains the same qualification about required expertise.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NN filters, trained on optimized semi-manual traces of neurites, can significantly outperform conventional filters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ANN-based filters, trained on optimized semi-manual traces of neurites, can significantly outperform conventional filters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological result using synonymous terminology (NN/ANN) without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: U-Net based filtering can virtually eliminate background intensity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: U-Net based filtering can virtually eliminate background intensity while also reducing the effective radius of neurites to nearly 1 voxel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the background-elimination claim and explicitly pairs it with the radius-reduction result, clarifying the combined outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: U-Net based filtering reduces the radius of neurites by 23% to nearly 1 voxel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: U-Net based filtering can virtually eliminate background intensity while also reducing the effective radius of neurites to nearly 1 voxel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication incorporates the quantitative radius-reduction into a combined statement about U-Net filtering, preserving the result but phrasing it together with background elimination.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: U-Net based filtering decreases intensity in the cross-over regions between neurites by 22%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Including an ANN-based filtering step can be beneficial for automated neuron tracing projects. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific quantitative result is not reported in the publication; instead the authors generalize the practical benefit of ANN filtering for tracing projects and lower the stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: U-Net based filtering reduces variations in intensity along neurites by 26%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Including an ANN-based filtering step can be beneficial for automated neuron tracing projects. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Quantitative details about intensity-variation reduction are omitted in the publication, which instead presents a higher-level conclusion about utility with reduced certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNNs reproducing single neuron firing rate motifs may not adequately capture important population motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNNs reproducing single-neuron firing rate motifs may not adequately capture important population motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this cautionary result verbatim, preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Even when RNNs recapitulate key neurophysiological features on both the single neuron and population levels, it can do so through distinctly different dynamical mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Even when RNNs reproduce key neurophysiological features on both the single neuron and population levels, they can do so through distinctly different dynamical mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same finding about distinct dynamical mechanisms with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An RNN consistent with a previously proposed dynamical mechanism is more robust to noise. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An RNN consistent with a previously proposed dynamical mechanism is more robust to input noise. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same (robustness to noise) but the publication reports a lower certainty (medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dynamics of the RNN are sufficient for it to generalize to a target switch task it was not trained on. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNNs trained to only perform a delayed reach task can generalize to perform tasks where the target is switched or the target location is changed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the generalization result in task-specific terms that match the preprint's claim and retains high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combined model demonstrates that visual information maps onto object meanings represented as activation patterns across features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combined model demonstrates how visual information maps onto object meanings represented as activation patterns across features. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same core demonstration with essentially identical wording and retained certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Visual layers explained activation patterns in early visual cortex, while perirhinal cortex activation was best explained by later stages of the attractor network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Visual layers explained activation patterns in early visual cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The portion about early visual cortex is directly restated; the perirhinal cortex element maps to a separate publication claim (see next alignment).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Posterior ventral temporal cortex activation was best explained by intermediate stages corresponding to initial semantic processing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Posterior ventral temporal cortex was best explained by intermediate stages corresponding to initial semantic processing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this mapping between intermediate network stages and posterior ventral temporal cortex activation without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results provide proof of principle of how a mechanistic model of combined visuo-semantic processing can account for pattern-information in the ventral stream. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combined model demonstrates how visual information maps onto object meanings represented as activation patterns across features. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts wording from 'proof of principle' to a more direct 'demonstrates' statement, clarifying and slightly strengthening the concluding claim while keeping scope the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both generalization across exemplars and generalization of object-related signals across time increase after 150 ms, peaking around 230 ms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both generalization across exemplars and generalization of object-related signals across time increase after 150 ms, peaking around 230 ms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this temporal generalization result verbatim, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Behavioral judgments explain the most unique variance in the response after 150 ms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Representations specific to behavioral judgments emerged rapidly, peaking around 160 ms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the finding to emphasize rapid emergence and a more specific peak timing (~160 ms), narrowing the temporal characterization while keeping overall confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results suggest a lower bound for the emergence of conceptual object representations around 150 ms following stimulus onset. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results suggest a lower bound for the emergence of conceptual object representations around 150 ms following stimulus onset. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding inference about a lower bound at ~150 ms is preserved verbatim with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proprioceptive system can decode high-level, multi-feature actions. (certainty=medium, type=hypothesis)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proprioceptive system is critical for high-level tasks such as action recognition. (certainty=medium, type=hypothesis)
alignments.rationale: Both claims state a role for proprioception in high-level action-related processing; the publication frames it as being 'critical for' such tasks rather than explicitly saying 'can decode', which is a clarification of emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial neural networks could robustly solve tasks related to character trajectory-decoding and action recognition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial neural networks can robustly solve both trajectory decoding and action recognition tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint result with essentially identical scope and confidence about networks solving both tasks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only the action-recognition trained models possess directional selective units, similar to those in the primate brain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Uniformly distributed directional selective units were found only in action-recognition-trained models and not in trajectory-decoding-trained models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert exclusivity of direction-selective units to action-recognition-trained models; the publication adds the detail of 'uniformly distributed' which clarifies the original observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model links tuning properties in the proprioceptive system at multiple levels to the behavioral level. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The networks' units show tuning properties similar to neurons in the primate somatosensory cortex and the brainstem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's claim about model linking tuning to behavior is expressed in the publication as evidence of tuning properties similar to biological neurons; the publication emphasizes the neural tuning similarity, clarifying the mechanistic/representational aspect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Action-recognition better explains the functioning of the proprioceptive system than the canonical trajectory-decoding hypothesis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Proprioceptive encoding is associated with higher-level functions such as action recognition. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint made a comparative claim that action-recognition better explains proprioception than trajectory-decoding; the publication instead states an association with higher-level functions (e.g., action recognition) without the explicit comparative superiority, so the claim is toned down and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pisces effectively predicts drug synergy and drug-drug interactions through augmenting the original dataset 64 times. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pisces is a novel machine learning approach for drug combination synergy prediction that augments sparse datasets by creating multiple views for each drug combination. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both claims attribute Pisces's performance to data augmentation; the publication frames augmentation more generally as creating multiple views rather than specifying the '64 times' factor, clarifying the method description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pisces obtained state-of-the-art results on cell-line-based drug synergy prediction, xenograft-based drug synergy prediction, and drug-drug interaction prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pisces obtained state-of-the-art results on cell-line-based and xenograft-based drug synergy predictions and drug-drug interaction prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same performance claims across the same tasks with equivalent confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified a breast cancer drug-sensitive pathway from BRCA cell lines in GDSC by interpreting Pisces’s predictions using a genetic interaction network. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: By interpreting Pisces's predictions using a genetic interaction network, a breast cancer drug-sensitive pathway from BRCA cell lines was identified. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same identification via interpretation of Pisces's predictions; wording shifts from 'we identified' to a report, but the substance is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with the identified breast cancer drug-sensitive pathway activated had substantially longer survival time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the patient survival association present in the preprint; this clinical survival result is omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Pisces effectively predicts drug synergy and drug-drug interactions through data augmentation and can be applied to various biological applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a broader concluding claim about general applicability of Pisces to various biological applications that is not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DF-CAGE is a novel machine learning-based method for cancer-druggable gene discovery. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DF-CAGE demonstrates excellent performance in discovering cancer-druggable genes using multi-omics data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's contribution claim about a novel method is reframed in the publication to emphasize demonstrated performance with multi-omics data; the publication shifts focus from novelty to empirical performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DF-CAGE identified 465 potential cancer-druggable genes among ~20,000 protein-coding genes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DF-CAGE identifies 465 potential cancer-druggable genes from approximately 20,000 protein-coding genes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the numeric identification result verbatim; there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The candidate cancer druggable genes (CDG-genes) can be categorized into highly reliable, reliable, and potential gene sets. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The candidate cancer druggable genes identified by DF-CAGE are clinically meaningful and categorized into known, reliable, and potential gene sets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims report categorization of candidate genes, though the publication shifts labels slightly (e.g., 'known' vs 'highly reliable') and emphasizes clinical meaningfulness, which clarifies the categories.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DF-CAGE reports druggable genes mainly based on the CNAs data, gene rearrangements, and mutation rates in the population. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DF-CAGE reports druggable genes primarily based on copy number variations, gene rearrangements, and mutation rates. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same methodological basis for reporting druggable genes with equivalent emphasis and wording adjusted to standard terms (CNAs -> copy number variations).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A 338-gene signature accurately identifies macrophage polarization states at single-cell resolution across diverse human diseases and murine pre-clinical models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A 338-gene signature accurately identified macrophage polarization states at single-cell resolution across diverse human diseases and murine pre-clinical disease models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core result about the 338-gene signature's accurate identification across the same systems with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 338-gene signature consistently outperformed conventional signatures in transcriptome-proteome overlap and in detecting disease states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 338-gene signature consistently outperformed conventional signatures in the degree of transcriptome-proteome overlap and in detecting disease states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the comparative performance claim with the same metrics and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Crowd-sourced genetic and pharmacologic studies confirmed that model-rationalized interventions trigger predictable macrophage fates. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Crowd-sourced genetic and pharmacologic studies confirmed that model-rationalized interventions trigger predictable macrophage fates. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same confirmatory crowd-sourced experiments and conclusions; the label shifts from 'conclusion' to 'result' but the claim content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings provide a universally relevant definition of macrophage states and a predictive framework for developing macrophage-targeted precision diagnostics and therapeutics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings provide a formal and universally relevant definition of macrophage states and a predictive framework for developing macrophage-targeted precision diagnostics and therapeutics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the adjective 'formal' to the claim, slightly clarifying the contribution while keeping the universal-definition and predictive-framework claims intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An excitable system composed of WNT and FGF signaling drives axial elongation through the induction of a signaling center in the form of neuromesodermal progenitor (NMP)-like cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An excitable system composed of WNT/FGF signaling drives elongation by inducing a neuromesodermal progenitor-like signaling center. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the mechanistic result about WNT/FGF-driven induction of NMP-like signaling centers driving elongation with equivalent content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ability of NMP-like cells to function as a signaling center and drive elongation is independent of their potency to generate mesodermal cell types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the independence from mesodermal potency as a finding; that specific functional-independence claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The instability of the underlying excitable system is suppressed by secreted WNT inhibitors of the secreted frizzled-related protein (SFRP) family. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Instabilities in the excitable system are suppressed by secreted WNT inhibitors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state suppression of instabilities by secreted WNT inhibitors; the publication presents it with higher certainty, effectively amplifying confidence in the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Absence of these inhibitors led to the formation of ectopic tailbuds and branches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The absence of WNT inhibitors led to ectopic tail buds and branches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same phenotypic consequence of inhibitor absence with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results identify mechanisms governing stable human axial elongation to achieve robust morphogenesis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results identify mechanisms governing stable human axial elongation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same concluding claim about mechanisms governing stable axial elongation, with slightly more concise wording but unchanged meaning.
