alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes quantification of dewetting factors (radius and hydrophobicity) rather than restating the general mechanistic observation, thus clarifying and operationalizing the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We quantify the combined influence of radius and hydrophobicity on pore de-wetting by applying molecular dynamics simulations and machine learning to nearly 200 ion channel structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combined influence of radius and hydrophobicity on pore dewetting has been quantified using molecular dynamics simulations and machine learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This is a direct restatement of the same methodological and quantitative result in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a simple simulation-free heuristic model that rapidly and accurately predicts the presence of hydrophobic gates. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simple simulation-free heuristic model has been proposed that predicts the presence of hydrophobic gates in ion channel structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same heuristic model but with stronger stated certainty and a firmer claim of predictive capability, so the claim is clarified and its confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This model enables the functional annotation of new channel structures as soon as they are determined. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model enables the functional annotation of new channel structures as soon as they are determined. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this application/conclusion directly with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method may facilitate the design of novel nanopores controlled by hydrophobic gates. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: The publication restates the model contribution and adds detail on the data sources used, clarifying but not changing the core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LASSIE was applied to 51 high-coverage genome sequences annotated with 33 genomic features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LASSIE model estimates the fitness effects of all observed and potential single-nucleotide variants based on polymorphism data and predictive genomic features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The specific dataset detail from the preprint (51 genomes, 33 features) is not explicitly reported as a standalone claim in the publication; the publication reframes the result around the model's inputs and may have omitted that exact detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The map of allele-specific selection coefficients constructed by LASSIE is informative about both human evolution and disease. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of classical population genetic theory with modern machine-learning and large-scale genomic data represents a powerful paradigm for studying human evolution and disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements link the method to insights into evolution and disease, but the publication couches the conclusion more conservatively (medium certainty), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The map of allele-specific selection coefficients constructed using LASSIE reveals pervasive weak negative selection against synonymous mutations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific result about pervasive weak negative selection on synonymous sites is reported in the publication but not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The estimated selection coefficients from LASSIE are highly predictive of inherited pathogenic variants and cancer driver mutations, outperforming state-of-the-art variant prioritization methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces predictive performance against pathogenic and cancer variants that was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis identified 1118 genes under unusually strong negative selection that are primarily expressed in the central nervous system or associated with autism spectrum disorder. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This gene-level finding appears as a new result in the publication and is not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine-learning algorithm applied to host gene expression profiles identified a diagnostic signature that can accurately distinguish culture-confirmed enteric fever cases from other febrile illnesses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning algorithm applied to host gene expression profiles identified a diagnostic signature that can distinguish culture-confirmed enteric fever cases from other febrile illnesses with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve greater than 95%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a quantitative performance metric (AUC >95%), clarifying the original diagnostic-accuracy claim without changing its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diagnostic signature identified a further 12.6% of culture-negative suspected enteric fever cases in Nepal as likely true cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying the diagnostic signature to a culture-negative suspected enteric fever cohort in Nepal identified an additional 12.6% as likely true cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result and numeric value are carried forward unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis demonstrates the power of data-driven approaches to identify host-response patterns for the diagnosis of febrile illnesses. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis demonstrates the power of data-driven approaches to identify host response patterns for the diagnosis of febrile illnesses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is substantively the same, but the publication states it with lower certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Expression signatures were validated using qPCR, highlighting their utility as PCR-based diagnostics for use in endemic settings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Expression signatures were validated using qPCR, highlighting their utility as PCR-based diagnostics for use in endemic settings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The validation claim is unchanged between the preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Folding-Docking-Affinity (FDA) framework folds proteins, determines protein-ligand binding conformations, and predicts binding affinities from three-dimensional protein-ligand binding structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Folding-Docking-Affinity (FDA) framework folds proteins, determines protein-ligand binding conformations, and predicts binding affinities from three-dimensional protein-ligand binding structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is identical across versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that FDA performs comparably to state-of-the-art docking-free methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that FDA performs comparably to state-of-the-art docking-free methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework serves as a starting point for integrating binding structures for more accurate binding affinity prediction. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework serves as a starting point for integrating binding structures for more accurate binding affinity prediction. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding perspective is carried forward verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model for continual learning in a recurrent neural network combines Hebbian learning, synaptic decay, and a novel memory consolidation mechanism. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model for continual learning in a recurrent neural network combines Hebbian learning, synaptic decay, and a novel memory consolidation mechanism. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact contribution claim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Memories undergo stochastic rehearsals with rates proportional to the memory’s basin of attraction, leading to self-amplified consolidation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific mechanistic claim about stochastic rehearsals and rates proportional to basin size does not appear in the publication; the mechanism is not explicitly retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model results in memory lifetimes that extend much longer than synaptic decay time and capacity proportional to a power of the number of neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of retrievable memories is proportional to a power of the number of neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the capacity-as-power-of-N claim (Q3) while separately stating extended lifetimes (Q1); the preprint's combined statement is split and the capacity part is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Perturbations to the circuit model cause temporally-graded retrograde and anterograde deficits, mimicking observed memory impairments following neurological trauma. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Perturbations to the circuit model cause temporally-graded retrograde and anterograde deficits, mimicking observed memory impairments following neurological trauma. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this result claim verbatim with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LSTMs can learn complex dynamics of the mutational time series governing tumor progression, allowing accurate prediction of the mutational burden and the occurrence of mutations in the sequence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LSTMs can learn complex dynamics of the mutational time series governing tumor progression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same core claim that LSTMs learn the mutational dynamics; the predictive aspects are consistent with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulation results of mutational data generated by the LSTM are statistically indistinguishable from the empirical data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simulation results of mutational data are statistically indistinguishable from the empirical data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result claim verbatim and with equal certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Passenger mutations are significantly associated with established cancer drivers in the sequence, and the genes carrying these mutations are substantially enriched in interactions with the corresponding driver genes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Passenger mutations are significantly associated with established cancer drivers in the sequence. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core association claim; the enrichment-in-interactors wording from the preprint is shortened in the published wording (core association kept).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interactions between driver genes and their interactors are associated with poor patient prognosis, likely conferring a growth advantage for tumor progression. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interactions between driver genes and their interactors are associated with poor patient prognosis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the association with poor prognosis but drops the speculative causal phrasing; the empirical result remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of LSTM reveals numerous additional conditional drivers and unknown aspects of cancer evolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Application of LSTM reveals hitherto unknown aspects of cancer evolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the discovery of 'unknown aspects' but omits the more specific claim about 'numerous additional conditional drivers,' resulting in a toned-down, narrower conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DS astroglia exhibited more-frequent spontaneous calcium fluctuations, which reduced the excitability of co-cultured neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DS astroglia exhibited more-frequent spontaneous calcium fluctuations compared with control isogenic astroglia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core empirical observation with similar scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neuronal activity could be rescued by abolishing astrocytic spontaneous calcium activity either chemically or genetically. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spontaneous calcium fluctuations in DS astroglia reduced the excitability of co-cultured neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication separates the mechanistic link (Q1) from the rescue intervention (Q2); here Q1 reiterates the effect on neuronal excitability while the rescue is expressed elsewhere (see next alignment).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results suggest a novel mechanism by which DS alters the function of astrocytes, which subsequently disturbs neuronal excitability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Suppressed neuronal activity could be rescued by abolishing astrocytic spontaneous calcium activity either chemically or genetically. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the findings as rescue of suppressed neuronal activity (Q2) and phrases the mechanistic conclusion more cautiously; the mechanistic suggestion appears as Q3 (see rationale).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our study establishes an all-optical neurophysiological platform for studying human neuron-astrocyte interactions associated with neurological disorders. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results suggest a mechanism by which DS alters the function of astrocytes, which subsequently disturbs neuronal excitability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the platform-as-contribution claim; instead it focuses on the biological mechanism, so the specific platform claim from the preprint is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed two-step approach for predicting active cis-regulatory modules (CRMs) outperforms existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our predictions are substantially more accurate than the best achieved so far. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts superior accuracy relative to prior methods, which matches the preprint's performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first step of the approach involves predicting a map of CRM loci in the genome using transcription factor binding data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state the first-step claim about predicting a CRM loci map from TF binding data; that procedural detail is not present among the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The second step predicts functional states of putative CRMs using few epigenetic marks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our two-step approach can accurately predict functional states in any cell/tissue type of all the CRMs in the genome using data of only 1~4 epigenetic marks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands this contribution into a broader claim about accurate genome-wide prediction of CRM functional states across cell/tissue types using 1–4 marks, thus broadening the scope of the preprint's second-step claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results suggest common epigenetic rules for defining functional states of CRMs in various cell/tissue types in humans and mice. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The epigenetic code that defines functional states of CRMs in various cell/tissue types is universal at least in humans and mice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication states the universality more strongly and with higher certainty than the preprint's cautious suggestion, effectively amplifying the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The main protease from SARS-CoV-2 is a homodimer that undergoes a symmetry breaking event when transitioning from the solid state to the aqueous solution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The main protease from SARS-CoV-2 is a homodimer that undergoes a symmetry-breaking event when transitioning from the solid state to aqueous solution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this empirical observation identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The subunits of the main protease present distinct conformations of the binding pocket due to the symmetry breaking event. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The subunits of the main protease present distinct conformations of the binding pocket due to the symmetry-breaking event. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the subunit-conformation result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Water molecules play a previously unrecognised role in triggering the transition of the main protease to an asymmetric state. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Water molecules play a previously unrecognized role in triggering the transition of the main protease to an asymmetric state. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps this mechanistic conclusion intact with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transition to the asymmetric state of the main protease is essentially irreversible. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The transition to the asymmetric state of the main protease is essentially irreversible, as established by enhanced sampling simulations and machine learning approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the irreversibility claim but adds methodological evidence (enhanced sampling and ML), thereby clarifying and reinforcing how the conclusion was established.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study presents a thick-tissue three-dimensional (3D) MERFISH imaging method that utilizes confocal microscopy and deep learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents a thick-tissue three-dimensional (3D) MERFISH imaging method optimized for thick samples. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution (thick-tissue 3D MERFISH method) with equivalent emphasis; differences are wording/phrasing only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 3D MERFISH was demonstrated on mouse brain tissue sections of up to 200 µm thickness with high detection efficiency and accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 3D MERFISH was demonstrated on mouse brain tissue sections of up to 200 µm thickness with high detection efficiency and accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated verbatim in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The authors anticipate that 3D thick-tissue MERFISH imaging will broaden the scope of questions that can be addressed by spatial genomics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 3D thick-tissue MERFISH imaging is anticipated to broaden the scope of questions that can be addressed by spatial genomics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same anticipated impact with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dopamine release dynamics in the dorsal striatum (DS) and ventral striatum (VS) are coordinated and correlate with discrete behavioral motifs during self-paced exploratory behavior in mice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dopamine release dynamics in the dorsal striatum (DS) and ventral striatum (VS) are coupled to behavioral outcomes during self-paced exploratory behavior in mice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases coordination with specific 'behavioral motifs' as a more general coupling to behavioral outcomes, broadening the phrasing while retaining the core linkage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Disruption of dopamine dynamics with cocaine randomizes action selection sequencing and disturbs the coordination between DS and VS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Disruption of dopamine dynamics with cocaine randomizes action selection sequencing and disturbs the coordination between DS and VS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this experimental result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinct dopamine dynamics of DS and VS jointly encode behavioral sequences, with DS modulating the stringing together of actions and VS signaling to initiate and sustain the selected action. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinct dopamine dynamics of DS and VS jointly encode behavioral sequences during unconstrained activity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the joint-encoding conclusion but removes specific mechanistic attributions about DS/VS roles, thus revising and narrowing the original mechanistic claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the effectiveness of a machine learning-based data analysis pipeline for linking dopamine release dynamics to behavioral sequences. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state or highlight an effective ML pipeline contribution comparable to the preprint claim; that specific contribution is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deficits in gross hindlimb function, skilled walking, coordination, balance, and gait are observed two weeks post-injury in rats with lumbar spinal cord injury. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combined loss of spinal interneurons in laminae V-VIII leads to significant deficits in hindlimb function, skilled walking, coordination, balance, and gait. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the deficits as resulting from combined loss of interneurons and extends laminar attribution to V–VIII (vs V–VII), representing a substantive revision/expansion of the original framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interneuronal damage to laminae V-VII in spinal L2-L4 correlates with hindlimb dysfunction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine-learning quantification confirmed that interneuronal damage to laminae V-VIII in spinal L2-L4 correlates with hindlimb dysfunction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication modifies the laminar range to V–VIII and adds machine-learning quantification to confirm the correlation, strengthening the evidence and expanding the anatomical scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Animals do not regain lost sensorimotor function three months after injury, indicating that natural recovery cannot compensate for loss of laminae V-VII neurons. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Animals did not regain lost sensorimotor function three months after injury, indicating that natural recovery mechanisms of the spinal cord cannot compensate for loss of laminae V-VIII neurons. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The long-term non-recovery conclusion is retained but the publication expands the implicated laminar range to V–VIII, revising the anatomical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deficits in coordination and balance remain after three months, highlighting the long-term impact of interneuronal loss. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Animals did not regain lost sensorimotor function three months after injury, indicating that natural recovery mechanisms of the spinal cord cannot compensate for loss of laminae V-VIII neurons. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same long-term persistence of deficits but frames it more conclusively, increasing certainty relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Newly developed machine-learning models can non-invasively classify injured animals by functional readouts equivalent to time-intensive endpoint histological analysis. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine-learning quantification confirmed that interneuronal damage to laminae V-VIII in spinal L2-L4 correlates with hindlimb dysfunction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports ML-based quantification confirming correlations but does not claim full non-invasive classification equivalence to endpoint histology; the contribution is thus narrowed and presented more cautiously.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Random forest can predict predator-prey interactions for birds and mammals using ecomorphological or phylogenetic traits. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Random forest can predict predator–prey interactions for birds and mammals using ecomorphological or phylogenetic traits. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological result with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Random forest correctly predicted up to 88% and 67% of interactions and non-interactions in the global and community-specific datasets, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Random forest correctly predicted up to 88 and 67% of interactions and non‐interactions in the global and community‐specific datasets, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Quantitative performance figures are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: False non-interactions for focal predators in training data strongly degraded model performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: False non‐interactions for focal predators in training data strongly degraded model performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core finding is preserved but the publication lists a lower certainty level, indicating a tempered confidence statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides guidance on how to prepare training data to optimise machine-learning classifiers for predicting species interactions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides guidance on how to prepare training data to optimise machine learning classifiers for predicting species interactions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same methodological guidance claim with equivalent framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Heightened Ce metabolism predicted elevated defensive behavior in the presence of an unfamiliar human intruder. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Heightened Ce metabolism predicted elevated defensive behavior in the presence of an unfamiliar human intruder. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is carried forward verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Infant nervous temperament was identified as a significant predictor of peri-adolescent defensive behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Infant nervous temperament was identified as a significant predictor of peri-adolescent defensive behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same predictive finding with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was no link found between infant inhibited temperament and peri-adolescent EAc metabolism or defensive behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There was no link found between infant-inhibited temperament and peri-adolescent EAc metabolism or defensive behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The null relationship is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings suggest a connection between infant nervous temperament and the eventual development of anxiety and depressive disorders. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings suggest a connection between infant nervous temperament and the eventual development of anxiety and depressive disorders. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same inferential conclusion with matched certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach highlights the potential for ML tools to augment existing behavioral neuroscience methods. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach highlights the potential for ML tools to augment existing behavioral neuroscience methods. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological/conceptual contribution is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hybrid method developed in this study achieved a maximum AUC of 0.99 on an independent dataset for predicting transcription factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid method achieved a maximum AUC of 0.99 on the independent dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance and claim about the hybrid method's AUC are the same in preprint and publication with no substantive change in wording or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best alignment-free model obtained an AUC of 0.97 on the independent dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best alignment-free model achieved an AUC of 0.97 on an independent dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported AUC for the alignment-free model is identical between versions, preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method performs better than existing methods for predicting transcription factors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method performs better than existing methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert superior performance of the proposed method; publication wording is slightly shorter but retains the same claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Alignment-based methods show excellent performance but are unable to cover all transcription factors due to no-hits. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The alignment-based method was unable to cover all transcription factors due to instances of no hits. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation about alignment-based approaches failing to cover some TFs due to no-hits is preserved with essentially the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identify new co-component signal transduction systems in enteric bacteria that likely regulate virulence. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A fast-evolving superfamily of co-component systems in enteric bacteria is identified based on the domain and operon organization of VtrA/VtrC. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on identification and organization of a fast-evolving superfamily, whereas the preprint emphasized likely regulation of virulence; the assertion about virulence is toned down and the claim is presented more conservatively.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The heterodimeric VtrA/VtrC periplasmic bile acid receptor controlling Vibrio parahaemolyticus T3SS2 is a distant homolog of the ToxR/ToxS master regulator of virulence. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The membrane-tethered periplasmic bile acid sensor activates the Vibrio parahaemolyticus type III secretion system through an obligate heterodimer of two proteins encoded by partially overlapping VtrA and VtrC genes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the heterodimer/T3SS2 functional mechanism but drops the explicit claim of distant homology to ToxR/ToxS, shifting the emphasis to mechanistic activation rather than evolutionary relationship.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurate machine learning-based fold predictions for the candidate co-components support their homology in the twilight zone of rapidly evolving sequence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Accurate machine learning–based fold predictions for the candidate co-components support their homology in the twilight zone of rapidly evolving sequences. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core claim that ML fold predictions support homology is retained verbatim, though the publication assigns a slightly lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structure prediction supports the divergent evolution of VtrA/VtrC-like co-component signal transduction systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A fast-evolving superfamily of co-component systems in enteric bacteria is identified based on the domain and operon organization of VtrA/VtrC. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both statements point to rapid/divergent evolution and a related superfamily; the publication frames this as identification based on domain/operon organization, clarifying the basis for the evolutionary claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a suggested role for lipid sensing in regulating virulence in enteric bacteria. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings provide mechanistic hypotheses about previously unrecognized lipid-sensing functions. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the tentative role for lipid-sensing but frames it as mechanistic hypotheses, a clearer albeit still cautious presentation of the same idea.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC) can be classified into two subtypes based on GPCR characteristics, which show significant differences in prognosis, gene expression, and immune microenvironment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study classified head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC) patients into two GPCR-based subtypes using machine learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report classification of HNSCC patients into two GPCR-based subtypes using machine learning; the publication statement is a direct restatement of the preprint's primary result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: S1PR4 is identified as a key GPCR that correlates positively with the proportion and cytotoxicity of CD8 + T cells in HNSCC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: S1PR4 emerged as a key regulator distinguishing the subtypes and was positively correlated with CD8+ T cell proportion and cytotoxicity in HNSCC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the identification of S1PR4 as a key regulator correlated with CD8+ T cell abundance and cytotoxicity with matching wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: S1PR4 expression is significantly associated with better prognosis in HNSCC patients and is accompanied by higher expression of T cell cytotoxic markers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings highlight S1PR4 as an immune modulator for favorable prognosis in HNSCC and offer a potential GPCR-targeted therapeutic option for HNSCC treatment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the association of S1PR4 with favorable prognosis but frames it more conservatively as a modulatory finding and therapeutic possibility, reducing the certainty level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: S1PR4 upregulation significantly increases T cell function in CAR-T cell therapy, indicating its potential in cancer immunotherapy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Upregulation of S1PR4 enhanced T cell function during CAR-T cell therapy, suggesting its potential in cancer immunotherapy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The experimental observation and the implication for CAR-T immunotherapy are preserved with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings contribute to the understanding of S1PR4 in anti-tumour immunity and suggest a potential GPCR-targeted therapeutic option for HNSCC treatment. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings highlight S1PR4 as an immune modulator for favorable prognosis in HNSCC and offer a potential GPCR-targeted therapeutic option for HNSCC treatment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements position S1PR4 as an immune modulator with therapeutic implications; the publication slightly reframes as a highlight/conclusion but retains the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FusionInspector accurately validates fusion transcripts in silico. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FusionInspector accurately validates fusion transcripts in silico. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The validation claim is identical between preprint and publication with unchanged certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biologically relevant fusions are enriched for relatively high expression of the fusion transcript, imbalanced fusion allelic ratios, and canonical splicing patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biologically relevant fusions are enriched for relatively high expression of the fusion transcript, imbalanced fusion allelic ratios, and canonical splicing patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The enrichment features for biologically relevant fusions are stated verbatim and retain the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FusionInspector helps identify and characterize numerous understudied fusions in tumor and normal tissue samples. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FusionInspector helps characterize numerous understudied fusions in tumor and normal tissue samples. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication tightens wording to 'characterize' (dropping explicit 'identify') and raises the certainty, amplifying the contribution compared to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clustering of recurrent fusion transcripts resolves biologically relevant fusions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FusionInspector helps characterize numerous understudied fusions in tumor and normal tissue samples. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes overall characterization of understudied fusions (which clustering contributes to); the wording shifts toward a stronger, broader conclusion about characterization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FusionInspector is freely available as open source for screening, characterization, and visualization of candidate fusions via RNA-seq. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FusionInspector is freely available as open source for screening, characterization, and visualization of candidate fusions via RNA-seq. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The availability claim is preserved but the publication assigns a slightly lower stated certainty to the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High-throughput experimentation has revolutionized data-driven experimental sciences and enabled the application of machine learning techniques. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quality of data analysis in high-throughput experimentation is heavily dependent on the quantification and accounting of random effects in the experimental data-generating process. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broad statement about revolution/enabling ML is reframed in the publication to focus specifically on the importance of quantifying random effects for data-analysis quality, narrowing the original generality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adequate calibration models that account for measurement noise are required for effective quantitative experimentation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adequate calibration models that account for both the input/output relationship and inherent measurement noise are essential for effective quantitative experimentation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim to explicitly include modeling the input/output relationship in addition to measurement noise, clarifying and broadening the calibration requirements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presented methodology improves the understanding and implementation of non-linear, empirical calibration modeling for quantification of analytes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presented methodology for non-linear, empirical calibration modeling improves the quantification of analytes and process modeling in life sciences. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the methodological contribution while explicitly linking it to improved analyte quantification and process modeling, clarifying practical impacts without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The software packages developed in this work enhance reproducibility and automation in data analysis routines in life sciences. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The software packages calibr8 and murefi enhance the accessibility of uncertainty quantification for various calibration tasks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication names specific tools and frames their benefit as improving accessibility of uncertainty quantification for calibration tasks, which is narrower than the broader reproducibility/automation claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Key process model parameters for microbial growth can be learned using maximum likelihood estimation and Bayesian inference. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of calibration models with a hierarchical Monod-like ordinary differential equation model allows for the learning of key process model parameters through maximum likelihood estimation and Bayesian inference. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the model context (hierarchical Monod-like ODE combined with calibration models) but preserves the core claim that MLE and Bayesian inference can learn key process parameters.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MOSAICA enables direct and highly-multiplexed in situ spatial biomarker profiling in a single round of staining and imaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MOSAICA demonstrates multiplexing scalability in detecting 10-plex targets in fixed colorectal cancer cells.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the multiplexing claim but specifies 10-plex detection in fixed colorectal cancer cells and omits explicit mention of a single-round protocol, narrowing and clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MOSAICA's analysis is strongly correlated with sequencing data (Pearson’s r = 0.9).
alignments.pub_claim_text: MOSAICA's analysis is strongly correlated with sequencing data, with a Pearson's r of 0.96.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the correlation claim but provides a higher numeric correlation (0.96 vs 0.9), clarifying and slightly amplifying the strength of the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MOSAICA demonstrates simultaneous co-detection of protein and mRNA in colorectal cancer cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MOSAICA enables simultaneous co-detection of protein and mRNA in cancer cells.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves co-detection of protein and mRNA but uses a broader term ('cancer cells' rather than 'colorectal'), broadening the scope while keeping the claim intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MOSAICA represents a simple, versatile, and scalable tool for targeted spatial transcriptomics analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the characterization of MOSAICA as 'simple, versatile, and scalable' for targeted spatial transcriptomics, so this broader concluding claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate a multiplication-free fixed-point spike detection algorithm with nearly perfect detection accuracy and the lowest complexity among studies we have seen.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate a multiplication-free fixed-point spike detection algorithm with an average detection accuracy of 97% across different noise levels.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same algorithmic claim but replaces qualitative 'nearly perfect' with a numeric average accuracy (97%), clarifying the performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By co-designing the system to incorporate statistically invariant features, we observe significantly improved long-term stability, with decoding accuracy degrading by less than 10% after 80 days of operation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: By co-designing the system to incorporate statistically invariant features, we observe significantly improved long-term stability, with decoding accuracy degrading by less than 10% after 80 days of operation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this claim verbatim, with no substantive change in content, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increasing the detection sensitivity improves decoding accuracy and long-term stability, which means the activity of more neurons is beneficial despite the detection of more noise.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increasing the detection sensitivity improves decoding accuracy and long-term stability, which means the activity of more neurons is beneficial despite the detection of more noise.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved in the publication with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reducing the spike detection sensitivity still provides acceptable decoding accuracy whilst reducing the bandwidth by at least 30%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reducing the spike detection sensitivity still provides acceptable decoding accuracy whilst reducing the bandwidth by at least 30%.
alignments.rationale: The content is unchanged but the publication downgrades the stated certainty (preprint: high; publication: medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings regarding the relationship between spike detection and decoding performance can provide guidance on setting the threshold for spike detection rather than relying on training or trial-and-error.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings regarding the relationship between spike detection and decoding performance can provide guidance on setting the threshold for spike detection rather than relying on training or trial-and-error.
alignments.rationale: This guidance/conclusion is present in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GP-age, an epigenetic clock for blood, uses a non-linear cohort-based model of 11,910 blood methylomes and outperforms state-of-the-art models with a median accuracy of ~2 years on held-out blood samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GP-age, an epigenetic non-linear cohort-based clock for blood, outperforms state-of-the-art models with a median accuracy of ∼2 years on held-out blood samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the main performance claim (∼2 years median accuracy); the preprint's sample-size detail is omitted but the substantive model performance claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Aging-related changes occur at multiple neighboring CpGs, which has far-reaching implications on aging research at the cellular level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Aging-related changes occur at multiple neighboring CpGs, which has implications for using fragment-level analysis of sequencing data in aging research.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the observation about neighboring CpGs but reframes the implication specifically toward fragment-level sequencing analysis, narrowing and revising the original broader cellular-level implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 30-CpG regression model achieves a 2.1-year median error in predicting age and shows improved accuracy (≥1.75 years) from sequencing data.
alignments.rationale: The specific 30-CpG regression performance claim is not represented among the publication claims provided, so it appears removed from the listed publication assertions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GP-age provides a compact yet accurate alternative to array-based clocks for blood, with future applications in longitudinal aging research, forensic profiling, and monitoring epigenetic processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GP-age provides a compact yet accurate alternative to array-based clocks for blood, with applications in longitudinal aging research, forensic profiling, and monitoring epigenetic processes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution and raises the certainty to high (from medium in the preprint), amplifying the confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neurons in a network of interconnected subregions of primate anterior cingulate cortex and basal ganglia predict the moment of gaining information about uncertain rewards.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neurons in a network of interconnected subregions of primate anterior cingulate cortex and basal ganglia predict the moment of gaining information about uncertain rewards.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spontaneous increases in their information prediction signals are followed by gaze shifts toward objects associated with resolving uncertainty.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spontaneous increases in their information prediction signals are followed by gaze shifts toward objects associated with resolving uncertainty.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pharmacologically disrupting this network reduces the motivation to seek information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pharmacologically disrupting this network reduces the motivation to seek information.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings demonstrate a cortico-basal ganglia mechanism responsible for motivating actions to resolve uncertainty by seeking knowledge about the future.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings demonstrate a cortico-basal ganglia mechanism responsible for motivating actions to resolve uncertainty by seeking knowledge about the future.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this overall conclusion identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Receptors that do not respond to an odor carry more information than receptors that do.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Receptors that do not respond to an odor carry more information than receptors that do.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents this conclusion verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Identifying what the odor is not is easier than identifying what the odor is.
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is easier to identify what the odor is not rather than what the odor is.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same conceptual conclusion with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method of elimination allows for accurate determination of odorants in a mixture and their concentrations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method of elimination allows accurate determination of odorants in a mixture and their concentrations.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A neural network realization of the proposed algorithm is constructed based on the structure of the olfactory pathway.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A neural network realization of the algorithm is constructed based on the structure of the olfactory pathway.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the neural-network implementation claim with equivalent content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Morphognosis model simulates the nest building behavior of pufferfish using a cellular automaton. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The morphognosis model simulates the nest building behavior of pufferfish using a cellular automaton. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about the Morphognosis model and cellular automaton simulation with effectively identical content and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model incorporates hierarchical spatial and temporal contexts to generate motor responses from sensory inputs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not mention the model's hierarchical spatial/temporal context mechanism; this specific architectural claim is omitted from the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An artificial counterpart of the pufferfish's behavior can be generated by treating its biological neural network as a black box. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An artificial counterpart of the pufferfish's behavior can be generated by decomposing its external behavior. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same conclusion but reframes the approach from 'treating the neural network as a black box' to 'decomposing external behavior', a clarification of the method rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The derived system has intrinsic value as an artificial entity and may help understand the biological system's behavior. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The derived system has intrinsic value as an artificial entity and may help understand the biological system's behavior. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about the derived system's intrinsic value and utility for understanding biology without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A targeted ANN-driven image synthesis method can predictably push the spiking activity of targeted V4 neural sites beyond naturally occurring levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Luminous power patterns can be applied to primate retinae to predictably push the spiking activity of targeted V4 neural sites beyond naturally occurring levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same experimental result but specifies the implementation (luminous power patterns applied to the retina) clarifying how the ANN-driven synthesis was realized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method achieves unprecedented independent control of the activity state of entire populations of V4 neural sites, even those with overlapping receptive fields. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method achieves unprecedented independent control of the activity state of entire populations of V4 neural sites, even those with overlapping receptive fields. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert the same strong result about independent control of V4 populations with overlapping receptive fields.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The knowledge embedded in today’s ANN models can be used to non-invasively set desired internal brain states at neuron-level resolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The knowledge embedded in today's ANN models might be used to noninvasively set desired internal brain states at neuron-level resolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion but softens the claim language from 'can' to 'might', indicating reduced certainty while keeping scope unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: More accurate ANN models would produce even more accurate control of neural activity. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: More accurate ANN models would produce even more accurate control. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same tentative conclusion linking model accuracy to control fidelity with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The end-to-end feedforward convolutional neural network can reliably classify the source and type of animal calls in a noisy environment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The end-to-end feedforward convolutional neural network can reliably classify the source and type of animal calls in a noisy environment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical result with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network can classify both the call type and which animal made it with a single pass through a single network using raw spectrogram images as input. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network can classify both the call type and which animal made it with a single pass through a single network using raw spectrogram images as input. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same technical claim and implementation details.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network increases data analysis capacity for researchers interested in studying marmoset vocalizations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network increases data analysis capacity for researchers interested in studying marmoset vocalizations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is carried forward identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system allows data collection in the home cage, in group housed animals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network allows data collection in the home cage, in group housed animals. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same practical capability; only minor labeling of claim type changed, not the substantive content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning methodologies are becoming part of the standard toolkit for experimental scientists across a range of disciplines. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning methodologies are becoming part of the standard toolkit for experimental scientists across a range of disciplines. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The broad conclusion about ML becoming standard toolkit is repeated verbatim and unchanged in emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tutorials provided are designed to be understood by investigators without any prior experience with machine learning, MATLAB, or coding. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The provided protocols for four ML methods are designed to be accessible to scientists without prior experience in ML, MATLAB, or coding. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that the materials are protocols for four ML methods, clarifying scope and format while preserving the accessibility claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The protocols include controls to test the validity of the results obtained from the machine learning methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The protocols include detailed explanations that allow investigators without a significant background in computer science or statistics to understand and apply ML techniques. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from explicit 'controls to test validity' to 'detailed explanations to enable understanding and application', indicating a substantive change in what the protocols highlight.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The aim is to equip experimental chemical biologists and other scientists with the knowledge required to use machine learning in their research. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The relationship between the scientific question and the choice of data and methods is emphasized to ensure meaningful analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the original broad pedagogical aim to a specific emphasis on aligning scientific questions with data and methods, altering the stated aim and narrowing its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interspecific differences in plant traits, particularly in biomass accumulation rate, maximum leaf area index, and elongation rate, were the most important factors explaining intercrop yield. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interspecific differences in plant traits within intercrops, particularly in biomass accumulation rate, maximum leaf area index, and elongation rates, were the most important factors explaining intercrop yield. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical finding with equivalent specificity and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The relationship between species yield and trait differences was symmetric for both intercropped species. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The relationship between species yield and trait differences was symmetric for both intercropped species. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is preserved exactly; only the claim labeling changed from 'conclusion' to 'result' without altering substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These relationships were scale-dependent, with trends observed at the aggregate level not always consistent at the level of individual experiments. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These relationships were scale-dependent, with trends observed at the aggregate level not always consistent at the level of individual experiments. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same result about scale dependence without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Farmers and researchers need to consider trade-offs when designing intercropping systems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the importance of considering trade-offs when designing intercropping systems for practical applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the trade-off recommendation but frames it specifically in the context of practical applications, narrowing the original more general exhortation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the value of combining machine learning with ecological knowledge to gain insights into complex agricultural systems from aggregated datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the value of combining machine learning with ecological knowledge to gain insights into complex agricultural systems from aggregated datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim about combining machine learning and ecological knowledge without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new AS enzyme with arylamidase activity from a urine isolate, Lacticaseibacillus rhamnosus, was biochemically characterized and demonstrated its capability to hydrolyze pharmaceuticals and other micropollutants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Urine-derived microbial Amidase signature (AS) enzymes have the potential to catalyze the biotransformation of organic micropollutants.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the experimental result more generally as a potential of urine-derived AS enzymes rather than the specific biochemical demonstration on a single isolate; this broadens scope while softening the asserted capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sixteen out of the forty enzymes tested showed activity on at least one substrate and exhibited diverse substrate specificities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified that the most promiscuous AS enzymes were active on nine different substrates, while 16 enzymes exhibited activity on at least one substrate.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative result and adds an additional detail about the most promiscuous enzymes, but the original claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chemical and amino acid features predictive of arylamidase biotransformations were identified using a gradient boosting machine learning model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four identified amino acid features were located in close proximity to the substrate tunnel entrance, which may influence enzyme activity.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides more specific information about the amino acid features (number and location) that the preprint reported as identified by ML, clarifying rather than reversing the finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Key chemical features from the substrates included the molecular weight of the amide carbonyl substituent and the number of charges in the molecule.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Key chemical features associated with AS enzyme biotransformations include the molecular weight of the amide carbonyl substituent and the number of formal charges in the molecule.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same chemical feature findings with near-identical wording, preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work highlights the understudied role of urine-derived microbial arylamidases and contributes to enzyme sequence-structure-substrate-based predictions of micropollutant biotransformations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Urine-derived microbial Amidase signature (AS) enzymes have the potential to catalyze the biotransformation of organic micropollutants.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized the understudied role and methodological contribution; the publication reframes this as a more focused conclusion about catalytic potential, narrowing the original broader contribution emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed machine learning model classifies skin lesions on Tamanend’s bottlenose dolphins with a high mean average precision of 65.6%-86.8%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed machine learning model classifies skin lesions on Tamanend's bottlenose dolphins with a high mean average precision of 65.6%–86.8%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact performance metric and claim from the preprint without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: At the population level, the model accurately estimates a prevalence of 72.1% spot and 27.3% fringe ring lesions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model accurately estimates a prevalence of 72.1% spot and 27.3% fringe ring lesions in the population of bottlenose dolphins.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same population prevalence estimates reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lesion presence in individuals suggests a positive relationship between lesion presence and gregariousness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lesion presence in individuals suggests a positive relationship between lesion presence and gregariousness.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same association claim but downgraded certainty (publication labels it as low certainty), indicating a tempering of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individual-level analyses from the model predictions may be more sensitive to data quality, making some questions unfeasible to address.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individual-level analyses from the model predictions may be sensitive to data quality, affecting the feasibility of addressing some individual scale questions.
alignments.rationale: Both versions convey the same limitation about sensitivity to data quality and resulting feasibility constraints.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Object detection models on photographic data are reasonably successful and provide initial estimates on the health status of understudied populations of bottlenose dolphins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advancements in machine learning provide more efficient ecological monitoring methods for assessing wildlife health.
alignments.rationale: The publication places the specific result in a broader context about ML advances for ecological monitoring, expanding the original contribution claim to a more general framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hyperspectral proximal sensing can be utilized as a high-throughput, non-destructive screening technique to identify salinity-tolerant grapevine germplasm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hyperspectral phenotyping can enhance selection intensity, ultimately leading to increased genetic gain.
alignments.rationale: The publication connects the preprint's screening utility to downstream breeding benefits (selection intensity/genetic gain), broadening the practical implications of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PLSR was the most effective machine learning approach, demonstrating moderate predictive capability for chloride content with a maximum R² of 0.67.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hyperspectral reflectance data can moderately predict chloride content in grapevines with a maximum R² of 0.67.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the predictive performance (R² = 0.67) reported for chloride prediction; the preprint added that PLSR was the most effective method, so the publication foregrounds the same performance result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With PLSDA, predictions increased considerably, achieving an accuracy of up to 0.97 depending on the instrument used and the spectral data transformation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Classification-based prediction utilizing partial least square discriminant analysis effectively predicts classes with higher accuracies.
alignments.rationale: Both claims report that PLS-DA classification achieves high accuracies for class prediction; the publication conveys the same finding in general terms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The more expensive and sensitive device with a wider spectral range outperformed the more affordable, shorter-range device in predicting chloride content.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different devices and spectral preprocessing methods produced variations in prediction accuracies ranging from R² = 0.11 to 0.67.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the device difference as part of a broader range of device/preprocessing-driven accuracy variation rather than asserting a single-device superiority, clarifying the nature of performance differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When the prediction model was based on classes rather than chloride content, both instruments performed very well with minimal differences in prediction abilities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Classification-based prediction utilizing partial least square discriminant analysis effectively predicts classes with higher accuracies.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's observation that both instruments perform well for class-based prediction aligns with the publication's general statement about effective PLS-DA classification performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All frequency bands studied exhibited a pattern of clusterization based on anatomical regions of interest related to neurodevelopment and cognitive and behavioral aspects of addiction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All frequency bands studied exhibited a pattern of clusterization based on anatomical regions of interest related to neurodevelopment and addiction.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same clustering observation across frequency bands with equivalent scope and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified patterns showed abnormal electrophysiological activity, indicating a dysregulation in the development of core resting-state networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified patterns of electrophysiological activity represent a dysregulation in the development of core resting-state networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same interpretation of the identified patterns as indicative of dysregulated resting-state network development.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The clusters found maintained plausibility and robustness, demonstrating the effectiveness of unsupervised machine learning in analyzing electrophysiological activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The clusters found in the study maintained both plausibility in nature and robustness.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the plausibility and robustness of the clusters reported in the preprint; the claim about ML effectiveness is implied by the successful clustering but expressed slightly differently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models can accurately classify ED-treated versus control mDANs with high accuracy (0.92).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ML-supported high-content imaging can identify subtle subcellular phenotypic changes in mDANs that are indicative of ED effects.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Scribe and its graphical interface are easy to use, freely accessible, and fully open source. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: TrimNN reveals key associations between spatially distributed cell-type patterns and diverse phenotypes.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The development of SynapseNet enables novel data-driven insights into synapse organization and function.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A shallow hybrid long-short sequencing approach can match or surpass germline variant detection accuracy of state-of-the-art single-technology methods.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The minimalRNN model with 'model filling' outperformed baseline algorithms in predicting Alzheimer's disease progression. (certainty=high, type=result)
